_comment: The DBMS OPPE paper, transcribed from the IITM Seek portal (Jan 2026 - DBMS,
course BSCS2001). Question wording is verbatim from the portal, including its
own typos ('data of birth', 'number of members types') — a past paper should
read exactly as it did on the day.
The portal's submission instructions ('select Zip in the Choose Language
dropdown', 'click Test Run') describe IITM's own UI and are deliberately not
carried over; they would be wrong here.
durationSeconds, marks and bestOf are all editable data — nothing about this
paper's shape is compiled into the app.
title: OPPE — January 2026
source: IITM BS Seek portal — BSCS2001 Jan 2026, OPE SQL + Python-PostgreSQL questions

questions.title: Classrooms with a capacity over 50
questions.body_md: Write an SQL query to find the room number and building name of the classroom that has a capacity of more than 50 students.
questions.reference_sql: SELECT room_number, building FROM classroom WHERE capacity > 50

questions.title: Players whose name ends with 'a'
questions.body_md: Write an SQL query to find the player IDs of the players whose name ends with 'a'.
questions.reference_sql: SELECT player_id FROM players WHERE name LIKE '%a'

questions.title: Match dates outside a host-score range
questions.body_md: Write an SQL query to find the match dates where the host team score is not between 3 and 5.
questions.reference_sql: SELECT match_date FROM matches WHERE host_team_score NOT BETWEEN 3 AND 5

questions.title: Manager of team 'Arawali'
questions.body_md: Write an SQL query to find the name of the manager of team 'Arawali'.
questions.reference_sql: SELECT m.name FROM managers m JOIN teams t ON m.team_id = t.team_id WHERE t.name = 'Arawali'

questions.title: Members in each member type
questions.body_md: Write an SQL query to find the member type and the total number of members in each type. The output should be displayed in descending order of the number of members types.
questions.reference_sql: SELECT member_type, COUNT(*) AS number_of_members FROM members GROUP BY member_type ORDER BY number_of_members DESC

questions.title: Teams whose manager is outside a set of IDs
questions.body_md: Write an SQL query to find the team name and manager name whose manager IDs are not 'M0001', 'M0003' or 'M0005'.
questions.reference_sql: SELECT t.name AS team_name, m.name AS manager_name FROM managers m JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = m.team_id WHERE m.mgr_id NOT IN ('M0001','M0003','M0005')

questions.title: Players wearing jersey number 59
questions.body_md: Write an SQL query to find the player name, data of birth, team name and manager name of each player whose jersey number is 59.
questions.reference_sql: SELECT p.name AS player_name, p.dob, t.name AS team_name, m.name AS manager_name FROM players p JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = p.team_id JOIN managers m ON m.team_id = t.team_id WHERE p.jersey_no = 59

_comment: Q8 and Q9 are Python programs that connect to PostgreSQL. They need the
Python-to-Postgres bridge (Phase 5) before they can run, so they are held

questions.title: ISBNs published in a computed year
questions.note: Reads x from number.txt; year = L4(x) = 2000 + 4*x + 4; prints matching ISBNs space-separated.

questions.title: Main referee for a match date
questions.note: Reads a date from date.txt; prints the main referee formatted as 'Sapam K.' / 'Sarkar A. K.'.
